diarized_transcript:
[Agent] Thank you for calling Genshin Impact Player Support, this is Priya. How can I help?
[Caller] I'm trying to understand the weapon banner. I see this Epitomized Path thing and Fate Points and I'm completely lost. I don't want to pull blind and waste primogems.
[Agent] Completely fair — the weapon banner has the most complicated rules in the game, and it's worth a few minutes to understand before you commit primogems. Let me pull your account and walk through it with your numbers. UID please?
[Caller] 832118007.
[Agent] Thank you, Yuki. Confirmed — AR60, Asia. Let's break this down. The weapon banner works like the character banner with two twists. First, when you get a 5-star, there's a 75% chance it's one of the two featured weapons and 25% chance it's a standard 5-star. Second, even if you hit a featured weapon, it's a 50/50 between the two — so without Epitomized Path, you could get the one you didn't want.
[Caller] Right, that's the part that scares me.
[Agent] This is exactly what Epitomized Path solves. Before you pull, you select your desired weapon as your 'Course.' Every time you pull a 5-star that is not your chosen one, you gain 1 Fate Point. At 2 Fate Points, the next 5-star is guaranteed to be your chosen weapon. Fate Points reset only if you switch Course or the banner ends.
[Caller] So worst case I get the wrong weapon twice, then the third is locked in.
[Agent] Exactly. Hard pity is 80 on weapon banner, soft pity starts around 63. So the absolute worst-case for your chosen weapon is roughly 240 pulls, though realistically most players get it in 80 to 160. If you're going in, commit enough primogems for that range and select your Course before the first pull.
[Caller] That's super clear. One more — what if I don't set the Course?
[Agent] Then you're fully at the mercy of the 50/50 on featured weapons, which nobody recommends. Always set your Course. I'll email you a one-pager summary covering Epitomized Path, Fate Points, soft and hard pity for weapons.
[Caller] Perfect. Thank you Priya, that saved me a ton of regret.
[Agent] Happy to help, Yuki. Good luck with the pull — hope it lands early!
